task_completion.description: Navigate to the exercise creation form in the wger fitness manager.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add Exercise' or 'New Exercise' form is visible in the web browser.
The text 'Sled Push' is visible in the 'Name' input field.
'Legs' is selected in the 'Category' dropdown menu.
The text 'Push a weighted sled across a flat surface. Drive through your legs while keeping your body at a 45-degree angle.' is visible in the 'Description' text area.
'Gluteus maximus' is selected or checked in the primary muscles section.
'Gastrocnemius' (or 'Calves') is selected or checked in the secondary muscles section.
A success message indicating the exercise was created, or the exercise details page for 'Sled Push' is displayed showing the entered information.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the wger application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The wger web interface is visible in a browser window, rather than terminal commands (e.g., curl) or API clients being used to create the exercise.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual wger UI elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Dropdowns, text fields, and buttons native to the wger web application are being used, not a text editor or mock HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI to manipulate the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing SQL commands, python scripts, or database clients are used to insert the exercise record.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected exercise name is 'Sled Push', category is 'Legs', primary muscle is 'Gluteus maximus', and secondary muscle is 'Gastrocnemius'. Note: The wger application may display or map 'Gastrocnemius' to 'Calves' (its English name attribute). If the UI shows 'Calves' for the secondary muscle, it must be considered correct.
